description:
**1. Overall Layout**
The figure is a clean, publication-ready horizontal bar chart set against a pure white background, embodying a minimalist scientific aesthetic. The layout follows a top-to-bottom categorical reading order along the left Y-axis, extending left-to-right to display quantitative data along the bottom X-axis. The design utilizes intentional whitespace to maintain a crisp, uncluttered appearance suitable for a top-tier academic conference.

**2. Axes and Labels**
*   **Y-axis (Vertical):** A thin, solid charcoal grey line. It features three evenly spaced categorical ticks from top to bottom. Left-aligned to these ticks are the exact verbatim labels, written in a crisp, neutral sans-serif font:
    *   Top: "TrustScore-Learned (ours)"
    *   Middle: "SBERT"
    *   Bottom: "TrustScore-Uniform (ablation)"
*   **X-axis (Horizontal):** A thin, solid charcoal grey line bounding the bottom of the chart, representing a numerical range from 0.0 to 0.4.
    *   **Tick Marks:** Explicit ticks and numerical labels are placed at "0.0", "0.1", "0.2", "0.3", and "0.4".
    *   **Axis Title:** Centered below the numerical ticks, the exact text reads: "Score gap = mean(gold) minus mean(distractor)".

**3. Components (Data Bars and Error Whiskers)**
Three horizontal rectangular bars originate from the Y-axis and extend to the right. They are rendered as flat, 2D shapes with sharp, precise corners, utilizing solid fills without gradients, borders, or 3D effects to ensure maximum clarity.
*   **Top Bar:** Corresponds to the "TrustScore-Learned (ours)" category. It is filled with a solid, deep muted teal to draw focus as the primary proposed method. It extends to exactly 0.349 on the X-axis. Just outside the right tip of the bar, the exact text "0.349" is printed in a matching sans-serif font. A thin, black horizontal line (whisker) is centered at the tip of the bar, representing a standard deviation of ±0.016.
*   **Middle Bar:** Corresponds to the "SBERT" category. It is filled with a solid, neutral medium slate grey to represent the baseline. It extends to exactly 0.170 on the X-axis. Just outside the right tip of the bar, the exact text "0.170" is printed. A thin, black horizontal whisker is centered at the tip, representing a standard deviation of ±0.009.
*   **Bottom Bar:** Corresponds to the "TrustScore-Uniform (ablation)" category. It is filled with a solid, soft warm coral. It extends to exactly 0.072 on the X-axis. Just outside the right tip of the bar, the exact text "0.072" is printed. A thin, black horizontal whisker is centered at the tip, representing a standard deviation of ±0.007.

**4. Extra Annotations and Groupings**
*   **Baseline Reference Line:** A faint, dashed, vertical light grey line originates from the X-axis at exactly the 0.170 mark. It extends straight up to the top margin of the chart area, visually slicing through the top bar to provide a clear performance comparison. At the very top of this dashed line, the text annotation "SBERT" is placed in a small, unobtrusive sans-serif font to clearly indicate it as the baseline reference marker.

**5. Visual Narrative**
The visual hierarchy immediately draws the eye to the deep teal "ours" bar, contrasting its superior length against the neutral grey baseline and the warm coral ablation. The dashed reference line serves as the critical visual anchor, instantly communicating the performance gap between the proposed method and the baseline without requiring the reader to scan down to the X-axis.

**6. Styling and Formatting**
*   **Background:** Pure white, maintaining a clean academic aesthetic with no decorative grids, dots, glassmorphism, or drop shadows.
*   **Color Palette:** Strictly muted, print-safe, and colorblind-aware. The palette relies on three distinct categorical colors: deep muted teal (active/proposed), neutral slate grey (frozen/baseline), and soft warm coral (ablation).
*   **Typography:** All text (axis titles, tick labels, bar values, and annotations) is rendered in a highly legible, neutral sans-serif font (such as Helvetica, Inter, or Arial). No handwriting, 3D bevels, or outlines are used. All text is perfectly horizontal.
*   **Line Weights:** The axes, reference line, and error whiskers utilize thin, consistent stroke widths in charcoal grey or black to maintain a crisp, precise appearance.
